title: PostgreSQL Work Claims, SKIP LOCKED, Leases, and Fencing · Execution Trace: Feed Go Fair Queue Order
summary: Multiple instances poll one source, row locks span network calls, expired workers commit late results, leases renew silently after ownership loss, and queue fairness is unknown. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go fair queue order increment in multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Define due, priority, tenant, publisher, and starvation policy and test busy, empty, skewed, and repeatedly failing queues.
Finalize success or failure only when source, attempt, token, and current lease match in one affected-row check.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
